question.text: What is the minimum net worth requirement for an AD Category-I bank to become a Trading and Clearing Member of the Currency Derivatives Segment?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text explicitly states: "AD Category I Banks are permitted to become trading and clearing members of the currency derivatives market... subject to fulfilling the following minimum prudential requirements: i) Minimum net worth of Rs. 500 crores." **The Trap:** Rs. 100 crores is the minimum net worth for a Stock Exchange (not the bank). Rs. 250 crores is the minimum Net Owned Fund for Standalone Primary Dealers. **Cross-Exam Context:** The full set of prudential requirements for AD Cat-I banks: (i) Net worth ≥ Rs. 500 Cr; (ii) CRAR ≥ 10%; (iii) Net NPA ≤ 3%; (iv) Net profit for last 3 years. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** "500 crores, 10% CRAR, 3% NPA cap, 3 years profit" — these four criteria for bank membership must all be memorized together.
